description: A designed text card on the same deep navy-blue textured (wood-grain-like) background ICE uses for its branded series. In the upper-left is the round white U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal (eagle with shield) beside three lines of white serif text reading 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'. A thin dark-red horizontal rule runs full-width beneath the header. Centered below in very large bold white serif capitals is the headline 'FOR THE RECORD'. Beneath that, in large centered white serif type spanning four lines, is the body statement: 'Contrary to rumors, ICE is not giving a $750 reward for tips in support of civil immigration enforcement objectives.' The layout is clean and authoritative with no photos or illustration. Messaging technique: this is a rumor-control / myth-busting graphic using official government branding to debunk a viral claim that ICE pays $750 cash for immigration tips; the formal 'FOR THE RECORD' framing and agency seal lend it the weight of an official correction. Target audience: the general public and social-media users who may have encountered the rumor, intended to set the record straight while reinforcing ICE's authoritative voice.
notable_text: FOR THE RECORD | Contrary to rumors, ICE is not giving a $750 reward for tips in support of civil immigration enforcement objectives.
